items.title: Transit open-payment reliability Release
items.description: Release of Transit open-payment reliability improvements aimed at moving Average boarding time and Transit platform ARR, anchored in multimodal interoperability, ticketing consistency, and settlement clarity across city partners and led through Transit Fare & Open Payment Engine with stronger best-fare and failure-handling logic. The quarter concentrates on account-based ticketing, multimodal settlement, and partner interoperability across urban mobility systems so the work reads like a realistic OKR-driven investment set for a company with roughly 250 product and technology people.

customerKPIs.expectedImpact: Decrease average boarding time by improving transit fare & open payment engine with stronger best-fare and failure-handling logic and removing avoidable workflow friction.

businessKPIs.expectedImpact: Increase transit platform arr by strengthening transit fare & open payment engine with stronger best-fare and failure-handling logic and making the core workflow more reliable end to end.

productBricks.change: Released Transit Fare & Open Payment Engine with stronger best-fare and failure-handling logic.
Released Device & Validator Management with better field visibility and maintenance-state control.
Released Transit Operations Telemetry with faster alerting on payment and device anomalies.
Released Settlement, Ledger & Revenue Reconciliation with clearer transport-payment exception workflows.

deliveryChannels.change: Deployed faster exception and rollout-control workflows in Transit Operations Console.
Deployed validator reliability and configuration updates in the device estate.
Deployed tap and revenue-health visibility in Transit Revenue Board.

items.title: Parking control tower convergence Release
items.description: Release of Parking control tower convergence improvements aimed at moving Paid session conversion rate and Parking platform ARR, anchored in multimodal interoperability, ticketing consistency, and settlement clarity across city partners and led through Parking Configuration & Policy Management with reusable tariff and permit models. The quarter concentrates on account-based ticketing, multimodal settlement, and partner interoperability across urban mobility systems so the work reads like a realistic OKR-driven investment set for a company with roughly 250 product and technology people.

customerKPIs.expectedImpact: Increase paid session conversion rate by strengthening parking configuration & policy management with reusable tariff and permit models and making the core workflow more reliable end to end.
Decrease violation resolution cycle time by improving parking configuration & policy management with reusable tariff and permit models and removing avoidable workflow friction.

businessKPIs.expectedImpact: Increase parking platform arr by strengthening parking configuration & policy management with reusable tariff and permit models and making the core workflow more reliable end to end.

productBricks.change: Released Parking Configuration & Policy Management with reusable tariff and permit models.
Released Payment Channel Orchestration with cleaner consistency rules across mobile, kiosk, and partner channels.
Released Access, Permit & LPR Control with stronger event handling and permit validation.
Released Enforcement & Compliance Workflows with better citation evidence and exception routing.

deliveryChannels.change: Deployed consolidated policy and enforcement workflows in Operator Control Console.
Deployed cross-channel yield and compliance tracking in Parking KPI Cockpit.
Deployed rule-consistent payment and access behaviors in Parking Operations API.

items.title: City intelligence operating model Release
items.description: Release of City intelligence operating model improvements aimed at moving Policy experiment decision cycle and Public-sector platform ARR, anchored in multimodal interoperability, ticketing consistency, and settlement clarity across city partners and led through City Mobility Intelligence with common planning metrics across parking and transport. The quarter concentrates on account-based ticketing, multimodal settlement, and partner interoperability across urban mobility systems so the work reads like a realistic OKR-driven investment set for a company with roughly 250 product and technology people.

customerKPIs.expectedImpact: Decrease policy experiment decision cycle by improving city mobility intelligence with common planning metrics across parking and transport and removing avoidable workflow friction.
Decrease average parking search time by improving city mobility intelligence with common planning metrics across parking and transport and removing avoidable workflow friction.

businessKPIs.expectedImpact: Increase public-sector platform arr by strengthening city mobility intelligence with common planning metrics across parking and transport and making the core workflow more reliable end to end.

productBricks.change: Released City Mobility Intelligence with common planning metrics across parking and transport.
Released Planning, Scenario & Outcome Modeling with faster intervention and outcome comparison.
Released Operational Data, KPI & Analytics Platform with reusable city KPI packs.
Released Enterprise Integration Layer with reusable city-system ingestion patterns.

deliveryChannels.change: Deployed shared policy, curb, and mobility dashboards in City Mobility Insight Board.
Deployed cleaner planning-data access in Mobility Intelligence Data API.
